Metadata.homeTitle: Precisar · Empower how you use technology
Metadata.homeDescription: Empower how you use technology. Home with hero, rotating verbs, ticker, and stacked sections. Precisar 2026.

homeConvoca.intro: Every day, more information is produced and circulated than we can process.

chile.stat2Label: of the citizenry does not understand what they read
chile.outro: In Chile, access is no longer the only gap. The challenge today is to strengthen people’s ability to understand, analyze, and usefully apply the information that runs through their everyday and civic lives—not only in the news sphere or solely in digital settings, but also in services, institutions, platforms, and other spaces where people need to find their bearings and take action.

propuesta.body1: Information arrives in fragments. AI answers before you finish your question. Media change faster than we can make sense of them. And yet, everyone already navigates all of that—in their own way, with what they have.
propuesta.body2: At Precisar we strengthen that capacity: so more people can interpret the information they receive and build their own meaning.

homePrograms.title: Programs
homePrograms.stackHeadline: Millions are connected. With judgment, many are still missing.
homePrograms.body: We design tools, content, and training experiences—in person and online—that strengthen critical thinking, digital security, and civic engagement.
homePrograms.stackLinkCiudades: Cities Connected with Purpose — Workshops and support for municipalities to strengthen digital skills across the community.
homePrograms.stackLinkHub: Hub Digital Consciente — Immersive digital culture experiences for all ages, adaptable to any space.
homePrograms.stackLinkAprender: Learn Digital: It's Never Too Late — A programme for older adults who want to connect with more safety and autonomy.
homePrograms.stackLinkPensamientoCritico: Media education for teachers — Tools and resources to help teachers bring critical thinking into the classroom.
homePrograms.stackLinkFuncionarios: Course for public servants — Practical digital skills training for people who work in government.
homePrograms.stackBotOndaManoDesc: For messages, chains, iffy links, and voice notes. It helps you read signals, understand what’s reliable, and use AI with judgment.
homePrograms.stackBotOndaCivitaDesc: For making sense of the public sphere: the state, laws, the economy, the environment, and recent history. Clear language, context, and data.
homePrograms.stackBotOndaProfesDesc: For teachers and education teams: activities, good practice, rubrics, and transparent use of AI in the classroom.
homePrograms.stackBotOndaEscuchaDesc: Onda doesn’t just answer. It can also gather questions, experiences, and real signals from people who use the chat, so the team can review them and better understand what’s missing from the explanations.

ciudades.title: Cities Connected with Purpose
ciudades.body: We run workshops and support institutions and municipalities in designing and implementing media and digital literacy strategies. Through participatory diagnostics and community workshops, residents gain skills to access reliable information, develop critical thinking, and participate confidently in the digital environment.

hub.title: Hub Digital Consciente
hub.body: Immersive digital culture experiences for all ages, adaptable to events, municipalities, and educational settings. Through posters, videos, and user-centred interactive applications, we explore topics such as AI, disinformation, and privacy—turning them into participatory experiences that encourage reflection on technology’s impact on society.

aprender.title: Learn Digital: It’s Never Too Late
aprender.body: A programme designed especially for older adults, with in-person and online workshops that combine practical device use, digital safety, and social activities—strengthening their autonomy and connection with family and community.

homeParticipa.stackHeadline: How do you get informed today?
homeParticipa.stackTagline: Your experience changes how all of us get informed.
homeParticipa.stackBody: We want to understand how you receive information today and how you would like to receive it: which formats help you, which tone builds trust, and what you need to understand better, access more easily, and share with more judgment. Your answers go straight to media outlets, institutions, and decision-makers so they can improve how they inform the public.

homeEducacionMediatica.stackHeadline: Understanding information is also a skill you learn.
homeEducacionMediatica.stackBody:
Four dimensions for reading the digital world
with more judgment, more awareness, and more autonomy.

homePrecisando.stackHeadline: How information circulates—and what effects it produces.
homePrecisando.stackBody: Precisando is Precisar’s editorial: articles and debate to read the digital environment with more context, judgment, and depth. Explore recent posts and categories.
homePrecisando.stackUnderConstruction: Under construction. Articles will be published here soon.

homeSaberes.stackHeadline: Knowing isn’t hoarding data. It’s knowing what to do with it.
homeSaberes.stackBody: Three collections of open resources—to reflect and broaden perspectives.

homeBotOnda.stackHeadline: Onda doesn’t just answer. It listens, too.
homeBotOnda.stackBody:
Onda en Mano, Onda Civita, and Onda Profes: three chat spaces to guide you without replacing your judgment.
Service in beta.

participaPage.title: How do you get informed today?
participaPage.lead: Help us understand what information is useful to you, what worries you, and which formats really work in your day-to-day.
participaPage.consultaBody: Share your opinion in the external form and check the associated privacy policy.

somos.metaDescription: We strengthen capacities to understand, assess, and use information with more judgment and meaning. Co-design with municipalities, education, civil society, and public services in Chile.
somos.heroTitle: We strengthen capacities to understand, assess, and use information with more judgment and meaning
intro: At Precisar we work with organisations that want to strengthen people’s relationship with information and promote a more conscious, useful, and constructive use of technology.

We work with municipalities, educational communities, civil society organisations, public services, media outlets, and territorial networks to translate a complex problem — the unequal capacity to understand, assess, and use information — into concrete tools, methodologies, and resources adapted to real contexts.
somos.principle: We don’t work from closed solutions. We start from context, listen to how people experience this problem, and design with each partner ways to address it with practical sense, clear language, and capacity to implement.

problem.title: What problem we address
paras: We understand information in a broad sense. It refers not only to news or journalistic content, but also to institutional, civic, digital or non-digital, educational, and practical information that people receive, interpret, and use to solve problems, take part in public matters, access rights, and operate in digital environments.

Today the issue is no longer only connectivity. Although connectivity has advanced, a deep gap remains in people’s actual capacity to understand, assess, and use information usefully in everyday and civic life.

That gap is not limited to news or social media. It also includes institutional, educational, practical, and service-related information: the kind people need to orient themselves, solve problems, interact with institutions, and make informed decisions.

When that capacity is missing, confusion grows, distinguishing reliable information becomes harder, communication between institutions and citizens weakens, and barriers to participating, understanding, and acting with judgment widen.

items.title: We co-design with context
items.body: We don’t apply one-size-fits-all formulas. We adapt methodologies, tools, and resources to the needs, languages, and capacities of each organisation and the communities they work with.

items.title: We translate evidence into action
items.body: We produce and organise useful evidence to understand how people get informed, what barriers they face, and which tools can be more effective in supporting them.

items.title: We test in the field
items.body: We design and implement experiences in real contexts, with concrete audiences and diverse conditions, to learn, adjust, and leave replicable models.

items.title: We transfer capacities
items.body: We aim for organisations to take ownership of methodologies, resources, and tools, integrating them into their own practices beyond a one-off intervention.

items.title: We combine clarity, technology, and public purpose
items.body: We develop open tools, accessible content, and applied experiences that help people better understand how information circulates and how to use technology in a more conscious and productive way.

items.title: Diagnosis and listening
items.body: Processes to understand how different audiences get informed, what difficulties they face, and what kind of support they need.

items.title: Methodologies for territories and institutions
items.body: Design and implementation of working models for municipalities, schools, organisations, and public services.

items.title: Open resources and tools
items.body: Guides, activities, explanatory materials, participatory experiences, and conversational tools adapted to different audiences and life stages.

items.title: Support for institutional adoption
items.body: Support to teams and organisations to incorporate this agenda from their own roles, capacities, and objectives.

items.title: Evidence for decisions
items.body: Systematising lessons learned and generating useful inputs to improve practices, programmes, and ways of communicating.

partners.title: Who we work with
items: Teams working with older adults, youth, or specific communities

outcomes.title: What we aim to leave behind
outcomes.prologue: Our goal is not simply to run activities. We aim for every organisation we work with to be left with:
items: Better tools to understand how their audiences relate to information
Usable resources adaptable to their reality
Capacities to sustain this work over time
A stronger connection between evidence, communication, and action

approach.title: Our approach to collaboration
paras: We believe that tools work better when they are born from real use, local context, and people’s experience.

That’s why at Precisar we understand collaboration as a process of joint construction. We bring evidence, methodological design, pedagogical experience, and tool development; our partners bring territorial knowledge, community reach, local legitimacy, and implementation capacity. That combination allows us to build responses that are more relevant, clearer, and more sustainable.

why.title: Why work with Precisar
why.prologue: Because we combine four capacities that don’t usually come together:
items: Useful evidence to understand how people get informed and what they need.
Applied methodologies to translate that evidence into concrete intervention.
Accessible resources designed for different audiences and contexts.
Institutional support so capacities stay in place.

cta.title: Let’s talk
cta.body: If your organisation wants to develop tools, methodologies, or experiences to strengthen people’s relationship with information and promote a more conscious, useful, and constructive use of technology, we’d be glad to talk.
